note: All 10 personas copied verbatim from lib/personas/roster.json; only the app-specific 'motivation' field added. Product: paste a multi-session multi-timezone agenda -> one shareable link auto-localized to each viewer's timezone, with add-to-calendar links, no account, all state in URL.

personas.name: Priya
personas.stack: terminal + neovim, GitHub, Kubernetes dashboards, Datadog, Linear, Slack
personas.temperament: skeptical of new tools; hates signups; will inspect network tab if suspicious; abandons anything slower than a CLI
personas.motivation: Helps run her company's internal on-call/incident-review syncs and an open-source project's contributor calls across US/EU/India. Lands here to paste a 5-session schedule and hand teammates one link instead of fielding 'what time is that for me?' — but she'll inspect the network tab to confirm nothing is phoning home.

personas.name: Marcus
personas.stack: VS Code, GitHub, Vercel, Figma dev mode, Storybook, Discord/Slack
personas.temperament: enthusiastic early adopter; shares tools in team Slack when impressed; notices janky CSS instantly
personas.motivation: Posts the schedule for his team's public 'launch week' livestream series in a Discord. Wants to drop one link that shows every talk in each viewer's local time; will judge the parser and the polish of the shared view harshly, and share it if it impresses him.

personas.name: Wen
personas.stack: BigQuery/SQL, Google Sheets, Looker Studio, dbt, GA4, Slack
personas.temperament: lives in data hygiene; distrusts tools that transform data invisibly; wants CSV in/out everywhere
personas.motivation: Coordinates a recurring global webinar series and distrusts any tool that silently mangles times. Wants to SEE exactly what got parsed (and what didn't) and verify a couple conversions by hand before trusting it with the audience.

personas.name: Tomás
personas.role: Operations analyst at a mid-size company
personas.stack: Excel (power user), Tableau, Jira, Teams, SharePoint
personas.temperament: pragmatic; IT blocks installs so browser tools appeal; wary of pasting company data into random sites
personas.motivation: Schedules cross-region all-hands and training sessions for a distributed ops team. A no-install browser tool appeals, but he's wary of pasting internal schedule details into a random site — the no-account/no-server claim matters to him.

personas.name: Dana
personas.device: MacBook in cafes; phone between meetings
personas.stack: HubSpot, GA4, Notion, Canva, LinkedIn Campaign Manager, Google Ads
personas.temperament: ruthless about time; if value isn't obvious in one scroll she's gone; screenshots tools she likes for the team channel
personas.motivation: Promotes a multi-timezone virtual summit and needs the agenda in emails and landing pages so APAC prospects don't miss sessions. If the value isn't obvious in one scroll she's gone; if it is, she screenshots it for the team.

personas.name: Jules
personas.stack: Notion, Buffer, Mailchimp, Figma (consuming), X/LinkedIn/Mastodon, Discord
personas.temperament: posts constantly, juggles platform quirks daily; allergic to anything requiring a login for a small job
personas.motivation: Runs a weekly community office-hours + AMA series with a globally distributed Discord. Posts the schedule constantly and is allergic to logins for a small job — this is exactly the 'one link everyone reads in their own time' job she does by hand today.

personas.name: Aisha
personas.stack: Figma, FigJam, Notion, Loom, Maze, Slack
personas.temperament: judges craft hard — spacing, copy tone, empty states; a clumsy UI loses her regardless of utility; advocates loudly for tools that feel considered
personas.motivation: Helps coordinate a design-community event track across timezones and will open the shared view as an attendee. Judges craft hard — spacing, copy tone, the localized cards, empty/error states; a clumsy UI loses her regardless of utility.

personas.name: Rob
personas.stack: Figma, Illustrator, Photoshop, Google Drive, client Slacks/emails
personas.temperament: does repetitive asset-prep grunt work weekly; price-sensitive; compares everything to 'I could do this in Photoshop in 4 minutes'
personas.motivation: Occasionally schedules client review calls and a small creator-community workshop across a couple timezones. Lower-frequency user; will weigh this against 'I could just write the times out myself' — value must clearly beat doing it by hand.

personas.name: Elena
personas.device: laptop + phone, half the day in meetings
personas.stack: Google Calendar (wall-to-wall), Slack, Linear, Google Docs, Zoom, dashboards she skims
personas.temperament: 30-second patience budget; uses tools between meetings on her phone; anything requiring setup is dead to her; recommends tools that save her reports time
personas.motivation: Runs a distributed team across 3 timezones and shares sprint-ceremony / planning schedules. 30-second patience budget, often on her phone between meetings — will likely open a shared link on mobile and judge whether it's instantly legible.

personas.name: Sam
personas.stack: Notion, Sheets, Slack, Zoom, Asana, Amplitude (skims), endless browser tabs
personas.temperament: synthesizes and shares constantly — exports, links, screenshots; values anything that makes him look organized; mild tech confidence, won't debug anything
personas.motivation: Organizes cross-functional roadmap-review sessions with stakeholders in different regions. Synthesizes and shares constantly and loves anything that makes him look organized — a clean shareable localized agenda is his kind of artifact. Won't debug anything that breaks.
